contents.value:
```abap
DATA lv_name TYPE string.
DATA lt_table TYPE TABLE OF sflight.
DATA: lv_a TYPE i, lv_b TYPE string.
```
**DATA** declares variables. Use `DATA()` inline declarations in modern ABAP.

```abap
SELECT * FROM sflight
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_flights)
  WHERE carrid = @lv_carrier.
```
**SELECT** reads data from database tables using Open SQL. Use `@DATA()` for inline declarations.

```abap
LOOP AT lt_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs>).
  WRITE: / <fs>-field.
ENDLOOP.
```
**LOOP AT** iterates internal table rows. Use `ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL()` for best performance.

```abap
CLASS lcl_handler DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS on_click FOR EVENT click OF cl_gui_alv_grid.
ENDCLASS.
```
**CLASS** defines an ABAP Objects class. Use `DEFINITION` and `IMPLEMENTATION` sections.

```abap
METHOD get_name.
  rv_name = me->mv_name.
ENDMETHOD.
```
**METHOD** implements a class method declared in the DEFINITION section.

```abap
TRY.
    lo_obj->do_something( ).
  CATCH cx_sy_zerodivide INTO DATA(lx).
    MESSAGE lx->get_text( ) TYPE 'E'.
ENDTRY.
```
**TRY/CATCH** handles exceptions. Catch specific exception classes.

```abap
READ TABLE lt_flights INTO DATA(ls_flight)
  WITH KEY carrid = 'LH'.
IF sy-subrc = 0.
  " found
ENDIF.
```
**READ TABLE** reads a single row. Check `sy-subrc` for success (0) or not found (4).

```abap
WRITE: / 'Name:', lv_name.
WRITE: / sy-datum, sy-uzeit.
```
**WRITE** outputs to the classic list. Use `/` for new line.

```abap
MESSAGE 'Success' TYPE 'S'.
MESSAGE e001(zmsg) WITH lv_val.
```
**MESSAGE** types: S=Success, I=Info, W=Warning, E=Error, A=Abend, X=Dump.
A **class** defines a blueprint for business objects with attributes and methods.
A **method** is a function that belongs to a class or object.
An **interface** defines a contract for classes to implement.
A **REPORT** program type processes data and produces output.
A **function module** is a reusable unit of code with defined interfaces.
An **internal table** is a dynamic data structure for storing records.
A **structure** defines a record layout with named fields.
A **transaction** is a dialog program identified by a transaction code.
An **exception** signals an error condition during processing.
A **domain** defines the technical attributes and value range for data elements.
**TYPES** defines a local type for use within the program.
**PERFORM** calls a subroutine (form routine).

```abap
DATA var TYPE string.
```
The variable-length character string type in ABAP, used for storing text data of arbitrary length. Unlike fixed-length character types (C), STRING dynamically adjusts its length and is the preferred type for text processing in modern ABAP.

```abap
MODIFY dbtab FROM wa | MODIFY dbtab FROM TABLE itab.
```
Updates an existing database row or inserts a new one if it does not exist (upsert semantics). When used with FROM TABLE, it processes multiple rows at once for efficient mass database operations. Also used with internal tables to update rows matching the table key.

```abap
APPEND wa TO itab | APPEND LINES OF itab1 TO itab2.
```
Adds one or more rows to the end of a standard internal table. APPEND wa adds a single work area as a new row. APPEND LINES OF copies all rows from one internal table to another. The system variable SY-TABIX is set to the index of the appended row.

```abap
cl_salv_table=>factory( IMPORTING r_salv_table = lo_table CHANGING t_table = itab ).
```
The SALV (Simple ALV) table class provides a simplified, object-oriented interface for creating ALV (ABAP List Viewer) reports. It handles column configuration, sorting, filtering, aggregation, and layout management without requiring a custom screen or container.

```abap
LOOP AT itab INTO wa.
```
Iterates over the rows of an internal table.

```abap
DELETE itab WHERE condition.
```
Deletes one or more rows from an internal table or database table.

```abap
INSERT wa INTO TABLE itab.
```
Inserts a row into an internal table or database table.

```abap
SORT itab BY field ASCENDING.
```
Sorts the rows of an internal table by specified fields.

```abap
CLASS cl_abap_typedescr
```
Type descriptor class. Provides RTTI (Run-Time Type Information) for all ABAP types.

```abap
CLASS cl_gui_alv_grid
```
ALV Grid control class. Used for displaying data in grid (table) format in GUI screens.

```abap
CALL METHOD obj->method EXPORTING param = value.
```
Calls a method of an object or class.

```abap
CREATE OBJECT obj TYPE class.
```
Creates an instance of a class.

```abap
RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE cx_exception.
```
Raises an exception that can be caught by a TRY-CATCH block.

```abap
CONCATENATE str1 str2 INTO result SEPARATED BY sep.
```
Concatenates two or more strings.

```abap
SPLIT str AT separator INTO TABLE itab.
```
Splits a string at each occurrence of a separator.

```abap
CONDENSE str NO-GAPS.
```
Removes leading/trailing spaces and reduces sequences of spaces to a single space.

```abap
MOVE-CORRESPONDING source TO target.
```
Copies identically named components from source to target.

```abap
FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs> TYPE type.
```
Declares a field symbol (pointer/reference). Provides direct access to memory.

```abap
ASSIGN itab[ idx ] TO <fs>.
```
Assigns a memory area to a field symbol.
